sections.paragraph:
πρωϊνός, ή, όν (PCairZen 207, 36 [III b.c.]; Babrius 124, 17 w. the v.l. προϊνων fr. cod. V. [L-P.]; Plut., Mor. 726e; Athen. 1, 19 p. 11c; LXX) pert. to early morning, early, belonging to the morning ὁ ἀστὴρ ὁ πρ. the morning star, Venus Rv 2:28; 22:16 (s. προϊνός for vv.ll.).—DELG s.v. πρώην.

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

Babrius Babrius , c. 200 a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. fr. = from

L-P. L-P. = ELobel/DPage; s. AnthLG

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

vv.ll. vv.ll. = variae lectiones (variant readings)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 892
